title: Walk the Huffman heap and compute per-symbol bit lengths
notes: Faithful port of zlib's gen_bitlen (trees.c §3). Walks state.heap from heap_max to heap_len-1, computing each node's bit length as parent-length + 1. Increments state.bl_count[len]. Detects overflow (any bit length > max_length) and runs the overflow-fixup loop that decrements overlong codes and increments next-longer shorter codes to restore max_length compliance. Writes final lengths back into desc.dyn_tree[i].len.

title: Construct an optimal Huffman tree from symbol frequencies
notes: Faithful port of zlib's build_tree (trees.c). Builds the priority-queue heap of leaf symbols with non-zero freq, then repeatedly combines the two lowest-freq nodes into a new internal node until one remains — the tree root. Guarantees at least two leaves in the degenerate case (the DEFLATE spec requires at least two codes in any Huffman tree). After the tree is constructed, calls gen_bitlen + gen_codes to produce final bit lengths and canonical codes.

title: Refill the deflate sliding window from the input stream
notes: Faithful port of zlib's fill_window. Slides the window when there's insufficient lookahead. Copies up to (2*wsize - lookahead) bytes from strm.next_in into the window at state.strstart + state.lookahead. Updates strstart, lookahead, block_start; refreshes ins_h for the first MIN_MATCH bytes in the fresh region; slides the hash chain tables via slide_hash when the window shifts.
